page: Microsoft Azure F2-1amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. It features 1 dedicated vCPU powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core, which classifies it as a memory-intensive resource.

This server type includes 236 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any GPU hardware accelerators, making it unsuitable for workloads requiring hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F2-1amds_v7 offers a high density of memory relative to its single dedicated vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high multi-core processing power. This hardware profile is suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and lightweight enterprise applications requiring local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure F2-1amds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 1 dedicated vCPU powered by an AMD processor and is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, representing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. Storage is supported by 236 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs included in this configuration. This memory-intensive profile is designed for workloads requiring significant memory capacity relative to compute power, such as small databases, caching systems, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
